page: Amazon Web Services c8ib.96xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 384 virtual CPUs mapped to 192 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. The processors operate at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated resource allocation and minimal virtualization overhead. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be fulfilled via network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps, facilitating high-throughput data transfers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8ib.96xlarge offers high compute density for workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration, avoiding the added cost of those hardware components. This profile is suited for compute-intensive workloads, large-scale parallel processing, high-performance computing, and network-heavy distributed applications.
description: Amazon Web Services c8ib.96xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 384 vCPUs across 192 physical Intel cores operating at 3.9 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a 2.0 GB per core memory allocation. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for high-density compute workloads, parallel processing, and network-intensive applications that require significant CPU capacity without the overhead of local storage or specialized GPU hardware.
